title: People using at least basic drinking water services, urban (% of urban population)
definition: The percentage of people using at least basic water services.  This indicator encompasses both people using basic water services as well as those using safely managed water services.  Basic drinking water services is defined as drinking water from an improved source, provided collection time is not more than 30 minutes for a round trip.  Improved water sources include piped water, boreholes or tubewells, protected dug wells, protected springs, and packaged or delivered water.

editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Thailand, Romania, New Zealand, while the lightest include Central African Republic, Congo, Dem. Rep., South Sudan; that contrast is the fastest way to read the urban basic drinking water pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Urban basic drinking water gives a city-infrastructure puzzle that is easier to read than raw water resources.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Basic drinking water access can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Rural basic drinking water can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Urban electricity access can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.

editorial.difficultyReason: Standard difficulty: the map has readable regional structure, but the best answer still depends on checking the unit and country anchors.
regionalSignals: Settlement maps reward checking dense city-states and large rural countries.
Urban and rural shares are mirror-image clues.

reviewNotes: Batch 3 promotion: clear urban infrastructure-access map with broad enough coverage.
Daily-worthy sparingly; do not pair with national safe-drinking-water in easier tiers.
